verdict.answer_first: A: opens with the bug signal, "most likely bug being an off-by-one"; B opens with process/meta, "doesn't need the full adversarial-review gate."
verdict.decisiveness: A is less scattered: it gives one concrete replacement snippet; B adds MAX_PAGE, magic number, ORM/raw SQL follow-up, and base-convention caveats.
verdict.fabrication: B invents irrelevant context: "full adversarial-review gate" and names "Postgres"/"Array.slice" though not given; A mostly infers from Express-style req.query.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to apply because its snippet is complete; B's snippet uses undefined "MAX_PAGE" and needs more decisions before it runs.
